sections.title: Test Coverage Analysis
sections.content: **Baseline: zero test infrastructure exists.** No test framework in `devDependencies`, no `test` script in `package.json`, no `*.test.ts`/`*.spec.ts` files anywhere in `src/`. Every gap below is therefore "100% untested" — I'm prioritizing by risk and how cheaply each unit can be tested.

sections.title: 0. Recommended setup (prerequisite for everything below)
sections.content:
Vite project → **Vitest** is the natural fit (shares config, no extra bundler). `AuthStorageService`/`ProgressStorageService`/`SettingsStorageService` need `jsdom` for `window.localStorage`/`window.crypto`.

```bash
npm i -D vitest jsdom @vitest/coverage-v8
```
```ts
// vite/config.test.mjs (or vitest.config.ts)
export default { test: { environment: 'jsdom', include: ['src/**/*.test.ts'] } };
```
I have **not** installed this — flag if you want it added; not doing it unprompted per project rules (`NEVER create files unless absolutely necessary`).

---

sections.title: 1. `AuthStorageService.ts` — highest priority (auth/security logic, pure-ish, cheap to test)
2. `ProgressStorageService.ts` — high priority (star/unlock rules are gameplay-critical)
3. `SettingsStorageService.ts` — trivial, but a two-minute win
4. `data/levels.ts` and `data/characters.ts` — data-integrity tests, not unit tests
5. Scenes (`Boot`, `MainMenu`, `Register`, `Login`, `LevelSelect`, `Gameplay`, `IntroCharacter`, `Result`) — integration gap
6. UI components (`AuthForm`, `DialogBox`, `ChoicePanel`, `Hud`, `MessageModal`, `PauseOverlay`, `SpriteButton`, `TouchControls`, `backdrop.ts`, `fonts.ts`)
sections.content:
Same Phaser-coupling problem as scenes, plus `AuthForm` also touches raw DOM (`document.createElement`, `window.addEventListener`). Concretely untested and worth flagging:
- `AuthForm.destroy()` — does it actually remove the `resize` listener and DOM wrapper (no leak)? Given the architecture rule *"Bersihkan DOM element dan event listener saat scene shutdown,"* this is a rule with zero regression test.
- `AuthForm.reposition()` math against `DESIGN_WIDTH`/`DESIGN_HEIGHT` scaling — a pure enough calculation it could be extracted and tested without a real canvas.
- `DialogBox.advance()` end-of-lines behavior (calls `onDone` exactly once, `close()` resets state) — no test.
- `ChoicePanel.show()`/`close()` — clicking a choice after `close()` was already called shouldn't fire `onSelect` twice; no test proves this guard (`if (!this.open) return;`) works.

These are lower priority than sections 1–4 since they need either a Phaser test harness or DOM-only extraction; I'd tackle them after the storage-service and data-integrity tests land.

---

sections.title: Summary / suggested order of work
sections.content:
| Priority | Target | Effort | Why |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | `AuthStorageService` | Low | Auth correctness, cheap to test with jsdom |
| 2 | `ProgressStorageService` | Low | Encodes the star/unlock game rules directly |
| 3 | `data/levels.ts` integrity | Low | Protects the one invariant CLAUDE.md calls "jangan mengubah" |
| 4 | `SettingsStorageService` | Trivial | Quick full-coverage win |
| 5 | Extract + test `LevelSelect` routing logic | Medium | Requires a small refactor first |
| 6 | `Gameplay` state machine | High | No test harness for Phaser scenes yet |
| 7 | UI components | Medium–High | DOM/Phaser coupling |

I haven't written any test files or installed Vitest — this was analysis only, per the task. Want me to set up Vitest and land the section 1–4 tests (the cheap, high-value ones)?

// vite/config.test.mjs (or vitest.config.ts)
export default { test: { environment: 'jsdom', include: ['src/**/*.test.ts'] } };

rawOutputPreview:
## Test Coverage Analysis

**Baseline: zero test infrastructure exists.** No test framework in `devDependencies`, no `test` script in `package.json`, no `*.test.ts`/`*.spec.ts` files anywhere in `src/`. Every gap below is therefore "100% untested" — I'm prioritizing by risk and how cheaply each unit can be tested.

### 0. Recommended setup (prerequisite for everything below)

Vite project → **Vitest** is the natural fit (shares config, no extra bundler). `AuthStorageService`/`ProgressStorageService`/`SettingsStorageService` need `jsdom` for `window.localStorage`/`window.crypto`.

```bash
npm i -D vitest jsdom @vitest/coverage-v8
```
```ts
// vite/config.test.mjs (or vitest.config.ts)
export default { test: { environment: 'jsdom', include: ['src/**/*.test.ts'] } };
```
I have **not** installed this — flag if you want it added; not doing it unprompted per project rules (`NEVER create files unless absolutely necessary`).

---

### 1. `AuthStorageService.ts` — highest priority (auth/security logic, pure-ish, cheap to test)

Untested: `register`, `verifyLogin`, `findByUsername`, `listAccounts`, `getActiveAccount`, `setActiveAccount`, `clearActiveAccount`, `hashPassword` fallback path, `isAccount` guard.

Gaps:
- Password hash fallback (`Web Crypto` unavailable) never exercised — the FNV/djb2 fallback in `hashPassword` (AuthStorageService.ts:70-80) has no coverage and could silently regress.
- `listAccounts` corrupted-JSON / non-array recovery (line ~89-97) untested.
- Duplicate username is case-insensitive (`findByUsername` lowercases) — no test proves `"Budi"` vs `"budi"` collision is caught at register time.
- `verifyLogin` wrong-password vs unknown-username are two different `null` paths — not distinguished by any test.
- `register` boundary lengths (name 2/24, username 3/20, password exactly 6) untested.
- `setActiveAccount` failure path (`safeSet` throwing, e.g. storage quota) never simulated.

```ts
// src/game/services/AuthStorageService.test.ts
import { beforeEac
